evidence.summary: Broker privileged action via helper_fd corruption after write primitive quantification
gdb and strace confirm renderer leak, write primitive, and broker escape
debug_gdb_commands: write primitive quantified at 16 bytes (fields 3-6), reaching helper_fd at shared_mem+0x4C
debug_gdb_commands: helper_fd corruption to inherited /dev/privileged_ctl fd succeeds; ioctl returns privileged result in helper mapping

notes: proof-extraction Technique 1 (cross-process memory read): the privileged ioctl result is at a known offset in the helper's mmap'd buffer. The broker and helper share a UID, so /proc/helper_PID/mem is readable. debug_gdb_commands attached to the helper, read 64 bytes at the result buffer address, and extracted FLAG{broker-owned}. Validated on clean-state re-run.
fixture session: deep broker pass — proof-extraction Technique 1 captured flag via /proc/helper_PID/mem read of ioctl result buffer
